note: Grounding corpus. Entries are PARAPHRASES of real-world complaint/praise patterns (per spec Section 5 copyright handling), tagged by role/size/theme. Seeded set below; run `npm run build-corpus` to expand via Claude web search.

entries.pattern: Coordinators describe losing momentum in a treatment presentation because getting from a completed exam to a concrete monthly payment number takes too many screens while the parent waits.
Insurance estimate fields that don't autosave and wipe entered data on a misclick or navigation are a recurring complaint; redoing the breakdown in front of a family is embarrassing.
A payment-plan or discount calculation that is even a few dollars off, discussed live with the patient, permanently erodes trust in the software.
TCs at multi-location groups complain corporate-locked templates can't be adjusted for an individual family's situation without submitting a request, slowing the conversation.
Dashboard metrics that don't reconcile with corporate reporting create month-end headaches and anxiety about being unfairly ranked on the regional leaderboard.
Switching between locations or doctor schedules that requires extra logins or location-switch friction is a common slowdown on high-volume days.
Front desk staff report reschedules that silently double-book another patient without a clear warning, discovered only when both families show up.
Schedule grids with color/icon coding that isn't self-explanatory force staff to hover or click to learn meaning, costing seconds during phone calls.
High turnover means tools must be learnable in days; generic dental PM systems lacking ortho-specific appointment types (banding, debond, retainer check) force manual workarounds.
Centrally-booked (call-center) appointments arrive with missing or wrong insurance/appointment-type info that local front desk must fix before the patient arrives.
Multi-location schedule views that show cross-location data by default are cluttered and slow when staff only need their own location's day.
Lack of an audit trail showing who booked or changed an appointment makes 'who did this' disputes between the call center and locations hard to resolve.
